message:
feat(cli,config): add automatic database recovery during issue mutation commands

Introduce update_issue_with_recovery() in cli/commands/mod.rs that
detects recoverable database corruption (malformed disk image, missing
index entries, duplicate schema) during issue write operations and
automatically rebuilds the SQLite database from the canonical JSONL
export before retrying the mutation once.

All mutation commands (close, defer/undefer, reopen, update) now route
through this recovery path on their first write attempt. The borrow
pattern in each command is refactored from extracting `&mut storage`
upfront to calling `storage_ctx.storage` inline, which avoids aliasing
conflicts with the recovery method that needs `&mut storage_ctx`.

config/mod.rs changes:
- Extend is_recoverable_database_internal_error() to detect "database
  disk image is malformed" and "missing from index" corruption patterns
  alongside the existing duplicate-schema-entry check
- Add OpenStorageResult::recover_database_from_jsonl() which rebuilds
  the database using the existing repair_database_from_jsonl() infra
- Add should_attempt_jsonl_recovery() convenience method
- Store bootstrap_layer and resolved_lock_timeout in OpenStorageResult
  so recovery can re-open the database with identical configuration
- Add test coverage for new corruption pattern detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
